message:
feat(packs): add cdn.cloudflare_workers pack

Implements protection for Cloudflare Workers, KV, R2, and D1 operations
via the Wrangler CLI.

Destructive patterns (8):
- wrangler delete (Worker removal)
- wrangler deployments rollback
- wrangler kv:key/namespace/bulk delete
- wrangler r2 object/bucket delete
- wrangler d1 delete

Safe patterns (12):
- wrangler whoami
- wrangler kv:key get/list, kv:namespace list
- wrangler r2 object get, r2 bucket list
- wrangler d1 list/info
- wrangler dev, tail
- wrangler version/help

Closes git_safety_guard-nys

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.2","title":"Simulate: evaluation loop + aggregation (counts, top rules)","description":"## Objective

Implement the core simulation loop that:

- evaluates each parsed command with the shared evaluator
- aggregates results into actionable summaries

## Aggregations (v1)

- totals: allow / warn / deny
- per-rule counts (`rule_id`)
- per-pack counts (`pack_id`)
- exemplar sampling per rule (keep top K shortest / first K occurrences)

## Determinism

- Sorting of summary outputs must be deterministic (count desc then rule_id asc).
- Sampling must be deterministic (first K occurrences by file order).

## Acceptance criteria

- `dcg simulate` produces stable output given the same input file.
- Aggregation respects limits (`--limit`, `--top N`, etc.).

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-1gt.8.1` (parser)
- `git_safety_guard-99e.3.1` (shared evaluator)
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### Tests
- Aggregation output must be deterministic:
  - stable sorting for “top rules” and “top packs”
  - stable bucketing for allow/warn/deny
- Unit tests should assert stable ordering and correct counts.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:15:42.58033159-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T23:46:57.799653069-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T23:46:57.799653069-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented evaluation loop + aggregation in simulate.rs with deterministic sorting and exemplar sampling. Updated cli.rs to use the new evaluation. All 107 tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:15:52.76100775-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:15:57.931365713-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.3","title":"Simulate: output formats (pretty/json) + redaction/truncation","description":"## Objective

Implement `dcg simulate` output formatting in both human and machine forms.

## Pretty output requirements

- Summary header (totals + elapsed time)
- Top rules table:
  - rule_id
  - count
  - decision (deny/warn)
  - 1-line reason (if available)
- Optional section: sample commands per top rule (truncated + redacted)

## JSON output requirements

- `schema_version`
- totals
- `rules[]`: `{ rule_id, pack_id, decision, count, reason?, examples[] }`
- `errors[]`: parse errors counts, malformed lines count

## Privacy

- Default to truncation in examples.
- Support `--redact` consistent with scan/explain.

## Acceptance criteria

- Output is stable for golden tests.
- JSON output is valid and additive-only for schema v1.

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-1gt.8.2` (aggregation)
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### Determinism
- Output formats must be deterministic and stable for diffing:
  - stable JSON schema versioning
  - stable sorting of aggregated tables
  - stable truncation/redaction rules","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:16:19.160687587-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T00:12:36.461516365-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-09T00:12:36.461516365-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented pretty/JSON output formats with redaction/truncation. Added --redact, --truncate, --top CLI flags. Created SimulateJsonOutput with errors object. All 22 simulate tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:16:29.475130683-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:16:34.556618726-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.4","title":"Simulate: fixtures + integration tests + detailed logging","description":"## Objective

Add tests that make `dcg simulate` trustworthy and easy to evolve.

## Test strategy

### Unit tests

- parser unit tests (format detection) live with `git_safety_guard-1gt.8.1`.

### Integration tests (this task)

- Run the compiled binary with `dcg simulate` against fixture log files.
- Assert:
  - exit codes
  - key summary counts
  - top rule ordering stability
  - redaction/truncation behavior

## Logging requirements

Test failures should print:

- which fixture file
- which line number caused parse failure
- the offending line (truncated)
- the expected vs actual summary

## Acceptance criteria

- Tests are deterministic across runs.
- Adding a new fixture regression is easy.

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-1gt.8.3` (output)
","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:16:52.270612219-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T13:56:01.692398617-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-09T13:56:01.692398617-05:00","close_reason":"Added 17 integration tests for dcg simulate covering: basic functionality, output formats (json/pretty), aggregation, redaction/truncation, limits, strict mode, determinism, decision log format. Commit 080db43.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:17:02.125735949-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:17:07.167409749-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-1gv","title":"[PACK] remote.ssh - SSH Pack","description":"# SSH Remote Access Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for SSH operations, particularly remote command execution.

## Destructive Patterns
### Remote Command Execution
- `ssh host 'dangerous-command'` - Detect destructive commands executed remotely
- Piping destructive scripts through SSH
- Redirecting scripts into SSH

### Key Management
- `ssh-keygen -R host` - Removes known hosts (security implication)
- Deleting SSH keys

### Config Operations
- Modifying ~/.ssh/config destructively
- Removing authorized_keys entries

## Safe Patterns
- `ssh -V` (version)
- `ssh-keygen -l` (fingerprint display)
- `ssh-keyscan` (key scanning)
- `ssh host` (interactive session - user takes responsibility)
- `ssh host 'safe-command'` (non-destructive remote commands)

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: remote.* (git_safety_guard-g5o)
- Keywords: ssh, ssh-keygen
- CRITICAL: Must detect when OTHER destructive commands are tunneled through SSH

## Testing Requirements
- [ ] Unit tests for remote delete commands via SSH
- [ ] Unit tests for piped script detection
- [ ] Unit tests for key management operations
- [ ] E2E test: `scripts/e2e_tests/remote_ssh.txt`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Remote destructive commands detected
- [ ] Key removal operations blocked
- [ ] Safe SSH usage allowed
- [ ] Interactive sessions not blocked","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:11:13.436955387-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T15:11:13.436955387-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gv","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.132964887-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gv","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.177154661-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gv","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.223350479-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gv","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.270713954-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gv","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.316989212-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gv","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.363582267-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gv","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:13.365141001-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gv","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:13.41138624-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1uf","title":"E2E: path normalization + quoting/encoding matrix","description":"## Objective

Expand E2E coverage around tricky path/quoting scenarios that historically cause false positives/negatives.

## Scope

- Absolute paths (`/usr/bin/git`, `/bin/rm`) plus quoted command words.
- Mixed quoting, escaped characters, Unicode edge cases, env assignments, and sudo/env wrappers.
- Encode tests for command substitution and pipeline contexts.

## Acceptance criteria

- New matrix cases added to E2E suite with clear expected results.
- Failures are easy to diagnose from logs.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1y6","title":"[PACK] cdn.fastly - Fastly CDN Pack","description":"# Fastly CDN Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for Fastly CLI and API operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### fastly CLI
- `fastly service delete`
- `fastly domain delete`
- `fastly backend delete`
- `fastly vcl delete`
- `fastly compute deploy` (with destructive flags)

### API Operations
- `curl -X DELETE` to api.fastly.com
- Service version activation (can cause outages)

## Safe Patterns
- `fastly service list`
- `fastly service describe`
- `fastly domain list`
- `fastly version list`
- GET API requests

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: cdn.* (git_safety_guard-wso)
- Keywords: fastly
- Note: VCL changes can cause immediate outages

## Testing Requirements
- [ ] Unit tests for service deletion
- [ ] Unit tests for VCL operations
- [ ] E2E test: `scripts/e2e_tests/cdn_fastly.txt`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Service deletion blocked
- [ ] VCL deletion blocked
- [ ] List/describe operations allowed","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:11:16.003637907-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T15:11:16.003637907-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1y6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:43.579216752-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1y6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:43.629856521-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1y6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:43.674595538-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1y6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:43.720542077-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1y6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:43.76776071-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1y6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:43.812852541-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1y6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:13.179162234-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1y6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:13.22592072-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-23g","title":"[PACK] backup.restic - Restic Pack","description":"# Restic Backup Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for Restic backup operations.

## Destructive Patterns
- `restic forget` - Removes snapshots
- `restic forget --prune` - Removes + reclaims space
- `restic prune` - Removes unreferenced data
- `restic key remove` - Removes encryption keys
- `restic unlock --remove-all` - Force removes locks
- `restic cache --cleanup`

## Safe Patterns
- `restic snapshots`
- `restic ls`
- `restic stats`
- `restic check`
- `restic diff`
- `restic find`
- `restic backup` (additive)
- `restic restore`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: backup.* (git_safety_guard-alc)
- Keywords: restic
- Note: forget without --keep-* flags is extremely dangerous

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Snapshot removal blocked
- [ ] Prune operations blocked
- [ ] Key removal blocked
- [ ] Backup/restore allowed
- [ ] Tests cover common Restic operations","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:32:50.784932143-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T18:39:02.206599212-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T18:39:02.206599212-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-23g","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:31.390900999-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-23g","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:31.43091172-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-23g","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:31.470796614-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-23g","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:31.510737012-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-23g","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:31.55195793-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-23g","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:31.591207458-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-23g","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:08.746487597-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-23g","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:08.792227725-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
@@ -250,7 +250,7 @@
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-mvh","title":"Define Ruby destructive patterns for heredoc scanning","description":"## Objective

Define AST-aware matchers for destructive operations in Ruby heredoc bodies, focusing on high-signal runtime behavior.

## Scope boundary

- Focus on destructive filesystem/process operations.
- Avoid broad “metaprogramming danger” patterns (e.g., `eval`) as default denies; they are common and not necessarily destructive.

## Pattern families (v1)

### 1) Filesystem deletion (high signal)

- `FileUtils.rm_rf(path)` / `FileUtils.remove_dir(path, true)`
- `File.delete(path)` / `File.unlink(path)`

Severity guidance:

- `Error` only for catastrophic literal targets.
- Otherwise default `Warning`.

### 2) Process execution with destructive payload (high signal)

- backticks with literal destructive payload: `` `rm -rf ...` ``
- `system("rm -rf ...")`
- `exec("git reset --hard ...")`

Important:

- Do not flag `system($VAR)` as deny; too broad. At most warn if combined with other signals.

## Testing requirements

- Positive fixtures for:
  - `FileUtils.rm_rf("/")` (catastrophic)
  - `system("rm -rf /")`
- Negative fixtures for:
  - `FileUtils.rm_rf("./tmp")` (warning at most)
  - `puts "rm -rf"`

## Acceptance Criteria

- Defines a Ruby pattern set aligned with severity taxonomy and focused on irreversible operations.
- Includes at least 5 positive and 5 negative fixtures.
- No default hard denies unless clearly catastrophic.
","notes":"CrimsonFalcon: taking over to align severity (catastrophic literal path blocks; otherwise warn) + add missing JS/Ruby positive/negative fixtures; will run full gates and push.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonFalcon","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:37:18.506057604-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T10:21:41.941491999-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T10:21:41.941491999-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented patterns + 5+ positive/negative test fixtures. All tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-mvh","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6sg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:37:35.430025823-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-mvh","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-f4f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:37:35.449812115-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-mvh","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-yza","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:51:30.957434297-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-nax","title":"[CATEGORY] search.* - Search Engine Pack Suite","description":"# Search Engine Pack Suite

## Overview
This category covers protection for search and analytics engines. Index deletion, mapping changes, or cluster modifications can cause significant data loss and extended downtime for search functionality.

## Packs to Implement

### search.elasticsearch
- **Why Critical**: Elasticsearch powers search for countless applications. Index deletion is instantaneous and permanent. Mapping changes can corrupt data.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `curl -X DELETE .../index` - Index deletion via REST
  - `DELETE /_all` - Deletes ALL indices
  - `DELETE /*` - Wildcard deletion
  - `POST /_reindex` with destructive dest - Overwrites destination
  - `PUT /_cluster/settings` (dangerous settings)
  - `POST /_close` - Closes indices (makes unavailable)
  - Kibana/DevTools equivalents
- **Safe Patterns**: `GET`, `_search`, `_cat`, `_cluster/health`

### search.opensearch
- **Why Critical**: AWS-managed fork of Elasticsearch with same risks. Many orgs migrated to OpenSearch.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**: Same as Elasticsearch plus:
  - `aws opensearch delete-domain` - Deletes entire domain
  - `aws opensearch delete-inbound-connection`
  - OpenSearch Dashboards destructive operations
- **Safe Patterns**: `describe-`, `list-`, `get-`

### search.algolia
- **Why Critical**: SaaS search with API-based management. Index deletion affects all applications using that index.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `algolia indices delete` - Index deletion
  - `algolia indices clear` - Clears all records
  - `algolia rules delete` - Deletes search rules
  - `algolia synonyms delete` - Deletes synonyms
  - API calls with `deleteIndex`, `clearObjects`
- **Safe Patterns**: `browse`, `search`, `list`, `get`

### search.meilisearch
- **Why Critical**: Growing alternative to Algolia/Elasticsearch. Same index deletion risks.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `curl -X DELETE .../indexes/` - Index deletion
  - `DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents` - Document deletion
  - `POST /dumps` (with overwrite)
- **Safe Patterns**: `GET`, `/search`, `/stats`

## Rationale
Search indices often take hours or days to rebuild from source data. During that time, search functionality is degraded or unavailable. Some search systems (especially SaaS) don't have point-in-time recovery, making deletion truly permanent.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: Tier 1 Epic (git_safety_guard-6ae)
- Blocked by: None (can start immediately)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All four packs implemented with comprehensive patterns
- [ ] REST API patterns detected in curl/httpie commands

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-nwu","title":"Bug: build dir removals incorrectly allowed by quick-reject","description":"## Problem

Commands like `rm -rf build`, `rm -rf target`, `rm -rf dist`, and `rm -rf node_modules` are being ALLOWED when they should be BLOCKED by core.filesystem.

This is a regression where the quick-reject logic (`pack_aware_quick_reject`) incorrectly allows these commands.

## Expected Behavior

With only `core.filesystem` enabled (and `safe.cleanup` disabled):
- These common build directory removals should be BLOCKED
- `rm -rf src` should be BLOCKED (this one works correctly)
- `rm -rf foo` should be BLOCKED (this one works correctly)

## Actual Behavior

- Build directory removals (build, target, dist, node_modules) are ALLOWED
- `rm -rf src` is correctly BLOCKED
- `rm -rf foo` is correctly BLOCKED

## Root Cause (suspected)

The `pack_aware_quick_reject` function in `src/packs/mod.rs` appears to be incorrectly classifying these commands as not containing relevant keywords in executable spans.

The unit test `blocks_rm_rf_outside_safe_dirs` passes because it directly tests the pattern matching without going through the quick-reject logic.

## Evidence

```
dcg explain "rm -rf build"
Decision: ALLOW
Pipeline Trace: quick-rejected (no keywords)

dcg explain "rm -rf src"
Decision: DENY
Pack: core.filesystem
Pattern: rm-rf-general
```

Both commands contain "rm" but only "src" is correctly evaluated.

## Files to investigate

- src/packs/mod.rs: pack_aware_quick_reject function
- src/context.rs: classify_command / executable_spans
- src/evaluator.rs: evaluate_command_with_pack_order_deadline_at_path

## Impact

E2E tests for rm -rf on build directories are failing.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:09:00.561192844-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T14:18:57.068568873-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-09T14:18:57.068568873-05:00","close_reason":"Bug was a stale binary issue. Rebuild fixed it. Added regression tests (pack_aware_quick_reject_rm_commands_not_rejected, full_flow_core_category_rm_commands_blocked). All 204 E2E tests pass."}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-nys","title":"[PACK] cdn.cloudflare_workers - Cloudflare Workers Pack","description":"# Cloudflare Workers Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for Cloudflare Workers and KV operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### Workers
- `wrangler delete` - Deletes Worker
- `wrangler deployments rollback` (can break things)

### KV
- `wrangler kv:key delete`
- `wrangler kv:namespace delete`
- `wrangler kv:bulk delete`

### R2
- `wrangler r2 object delete`
- `wrangler r2 bucket delete`

### D1 (Database)
- `wrangler d1 delete`

## Safe Patterns
- `wrangler whoami`
- `wrangler kv:key get`, `wrangler kv:key list`
- `wrangler r2 object get`
- `wrangler tail`
- `wrangler dev`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: cdn.* (git_safety_guard-wso)
- Keywords: wrangler
- Note: Workers are edge compute, KV is edge storage

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Worker deletion blocked
- [ ] KV namespace/key deletion blocked
- [ ] R2 deletion blocked
- [ ] D1 deletion blocked
- [ ] Tests cover common Wrangler operations","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:35:09.696316475-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T14:35:09.696316475-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-nys","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.4077607-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-nys","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.453653508-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-nys","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.502342657-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-nys","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.54949762-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-nys","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.596835677-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-nys","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.642547104-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-nys","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:13.456207928-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-nys","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:13.504506124-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-o15","title":"Design heredoc detection strategy","description":"# Design: Heredoc + Inline-Script Detection Strategy (Tiered)

## Objective
Design a **robust and performant** strategy to detect heredocs and inline-script constructs embedded inside a single shell command string (Claude Code `PreToolUse` Bash hook), so we can scan embedded code for destructive behavior *without* creating velocity-destroying false positives.

This spec is intended to be **implementation-ready** and self-contained.

---

## Terminology

- **Command string**: The exact `tool_input.command` string received by the hook. May contain newlines.
- **Heredoc**: Any `<<`, `<<-`, `<<~` form that introduces a delimiter-terminated literal block.
- **Here-string**: `<<<` form that introduces an inline string (single token / quoted string).
- **Inline-script flags**: `-c`, `-e`, etc. flags to interpreters that embed code directly in arguments.
- **Tier 0**: Existing pack-aware keyword gate (`pack_aware_quick_reject`).
- **Tier 1**: Microsecond-level trigger detection (RegexSet/fast scan). Must have **near-zero false negatives** for the forms we claim to support.
- **Tier 2**: Bounded extraction of embedded code payloads (delimiter-aware), with hard caps.
- **Tier 3**: Language-aware scanning of extracted payload (AST/pattern matching) under strict time budgets; **fail-open** on error/timeout in hook mode.

---

## Design Principles

1. **Fail-open for parser/timeouts in hook mode**: if heredoc extraction or AST scanning fails/timeout, allow the command and emit diagnostic markers (for `dcg explain` / logs).
2. **Tiered cost model**: Tier 1 should be *cheap enough to run on every command*; Tier 2/3 only run when Tier 1 triggers.
3. **False-positive immunity is a first-class goal**:
   - Tier 1 may be permissive (false positives OK) as long as downstream tiers quickly bail.
   - Tier 2/3 must minimize false positives by focusing on *executed code context*.
4. **Multiple embedded scripts must be supported** (multiple heredocs, or heredoc + `python -c`, etc.) with bounded total work.

---

## Scope: What We Support in v1

### A) Heredocs (Bash/POSIX-ish)
Supported operators:
- `<< DELIM`
- `<<'DELIM'` / `<<"DELIM"` (quoted delimiter)
- `<<- DELIM` (tab-stripping)
- `<<~ DELIM` (zsh indentation-stripping) **treated as supported trigger**, but extraction semantics may start as `<<-` equivalent (see below).

Supported delimiter tokens:
- unquoted: `EOF`, `__SQL__`, `END`, `DELIM123` (shell token without whitespace)
- quoted: `'EOF'`, `"EOF"`, `'$DELIM'` (we still treat the delimiter token as the literal inside quotes)

Supported layouts:
- heredoc on same line: `cat <<EOF\n...\nEOF`
- heredoc with pipes/redirections after: `cat <<EOF | sed ...`
- multiple heredocs in a single command string.

### B) Here-strings
- `<<< "string"` / `<<< 'string'` / `<<< $VAR` (we treat the token value textually; we do not expand variables)

### C) Inline scripts (not heredocs, but embedded code)
We treat these as “embedded payloads” too.

Supported interpreter patterns (command prefix mapping defined in `git_safety_guard-jfj`):
- `bash -c <code>` / `sh -c <code>` / `zsh -c <code>`
- `python -c <code>` / `python3 -c <code>`
- `node -e <code>` / `node -p <expr>`
- `ruby -e <code>`
- `perl -e <code>`

Notes:
- We do **not** attempt full shell parsing for all argument edge cases in v1; we do bounded heuristics that are correct for the common forms.

---

## Explicitly Out-of-Scope for v1 (Fail-Open + Diagnostic)

If Tier 1 triggers but extraction cannot confidently produce payload(s), we **fail-open** and attach a reason marker.

Out-of-scope cases:
- Fish shell heredoc syntax (different model)
- Heredocs inside complex nested command substitutions where token boundaries are ambiguous without a full shell parser
- Extremely exotic delimiter rules (e.g., delimiters requiring full shell expansion semantics)

---

## Tier 1: Trigger Detection (Fast, Safe)

### Goal
Return “maybe contains embedded payload” with **high recall** and very low overhead.

### Triggers (recommended)
Implement as `RegexSet` / `memchr`-gated regexes (see `git_safety_guard-t1rx`). Trigger if any of:

1) Heredoc operators (outside the trivial false-positive cases):
- `<<` (including `<<-`, `<<~`)
- `<<<`

2) Inline-script flags (coarse):
- `\b(?:bash|sh|zsh)\b[^\n]*\s-\s*c\b` (approx)
- `\bpython\d*\b[^\n]*\s-\s*c\b`
- `\bnode(?:js)?\b[^\n]*\s-(?:e|p)\b`
- `\bruby\b[^\n]*\s-\s*e\b`
- `\bperl\b[^\n]*\s-\s*e\b`

### Tier 1 false-positive policy
Tier 1 is allowed to trigger on:
- `echo "<<"`
- `printf '<<<'`

…but Tier 2 must cheaply reject when the operator is inside quotes / not a real heredoc.

---

## Tier 2: Extraction (Delimiter-Aware, Bounded)

### Goal
Extract a list of payloads:
- `HeredocPayload { operator, delimiter, quoted_delimiter, strip_mode, content_range, content_preview }`
- `HereStringPayload { token_range, token_text }`
- `InlineArgPayload { interpreter, flag, arg_range, arg_text }`

### Hard Limits (defaults; configurable later)
- `MAX_PAYLOADS_PER_COMMAND`: 4 (fail-open + diagnostic if exceeded)
- `MAX_TOTAL_EXTRACT_BYTES`: 64 KiB across all payloads
- `MAX_SINGLE_PAYLOAD_BYTES`: 32 KiB
- `MAX_LINES_PER_PAYLOAD`: 2,000

### Extraction Algorithm (heredoc)
We need a linear scan that approximates shell tokenization enough to identify real `<<` operators.

#### State machine (minimum viable)
Track:
- `in_single_quote` (`'...'`)
- `in_double_quote` (`"..."`)
- `in_backtick` (`` `...` ``) (optional v1)
- `escaped` (backslash escape outside single quotes)

Rule: A `<<` or `<<<` is only considered an operator when **not in any quote state**.

#### Parsing delimiter token
When `<<`/`<<-`/`<<~` found:
1. Determine strip mode:
   - `<<-` → `StripTabs`
   - `<<~` → `StripIndent` (treat as `StripTabs` in v1 if needed; record true mode for future)
   - `<<` → `NoStrip`
2. Skip whitespace.
3. Read delimiter token:
   - If next char is `'` or `"`, read until matching quote; delimiter = inner bytes.
   - Else read until whitespace or one of `|&;()<>` (shell-ish token boundary).
4. Record delimiter.

#### Finding delimiter terminator line
After locating delimiter token, the heredoc content begins **after the next newline**.

To find terminator:
- Scan subsequent lines.
- A terminator line matches when (after applying strip semantics to the terminator line only):
  - `line == delimiter` OR `line == delimiter + "\r"` (handle CRLF)

If not found within caps → fail-open + diagnostic `heredoc_terminator_not_found`.

#### Strip semantics
- `NoStrip`: keep content as-is.
- `StripTabs` (`<<-`): remove **leading tab characters** from each content line and from the terminator line check.
- `StripIndent` (`<<~`): v1 option:
  - implement as `StripTabs` initially (conservative), OR
  - remove leading tabs+spaces uniformly up to the minimum indentation across non-empty lines.

### Extraction Algorithm (here-string)
When `<<<` found outside quotes:
- Capture the remainder token following `<<<` up to newline/`;`/`|` boundary.
- For v1, do not expand `$VAR`; treat it as literal text.
- Provide payload text as the token substring (bounded to `MAX_SINGLE_PAYLOAD_BYTES`).

### Extraction Algorithm (inline-script args)
This is a separate path that does not require heredoc delimiters.

Approach:
- Perform a lightweight token split on the first line only (up to a cap, e.g. 4 KiB).
- Identify interpreter command name (token 0 or after `sudo`/env assignments).
- Identify `-c` / `-e` / `-p` flags and capture the following argument token as payload.

If tokenization is ambiguous (unclosed quotes, etc) → fail-open + diagnostic `inline_script_tokenize_failed`.

---

## Tier 3: Downstream scanning handshake (interface only)

Tier 2 yields payloads; Tier 3 consumes payloads.

Tier 3 must:
- run only when payload exists
- be time-bounded (configurable; default 25ms total)
- be size-bounded (Tier 2 caps)
- fail-open on timeout/error in hook mode

The Tier 3 integration point is `git_safety_guard-e7m` (blocked). This spec defines how Tier 2 feeds Tier 3.

---

## Diagnostics / Explainability

Tier 2 must be able to emit structured diagnostics for later explain mode:
- `triggered: bool`
- `payload_count`
- `fail_open_reason` (enum)
- `bytes_scanned`
- `timeouts`

Examples:
- `fail_open_reason=heredoc_terminator_not_found`
- `fail_open_reason=payload_too_large`
- `fail_open_reason=inline_script_tokenize_failed`

---

## Test Matrix (minimum)

### Positive detection + extraction
1. `cat <<EOF\nrm -rf /\nEOF` → 1 heredoc payload, delimiter `EOF`.
2. `cat <<'EOF'\nrm -rf /\nEOF` → quoted delimiter.
3. `cat <<-EOF\n\trm -rf /\n\tEOF` → StripTabs semantics.
4. `cat <<~EOF\n  rm -rf /\n  EOF` → StripIndent recorded (v1 may treat like tabs; still extract).
5. `cat <<< "rm -rf /"` → here-string payload.
6. `python -c "import os; os.system('rm -rf /')"` → inline payload.
7. `node -e "require('fs').rmSync('/')"` → inline payload.

### Multiple payloads
8. `cat <<A\nrm -rf /\nA; cat <<B\nDROP TABLE x;\nB` → 2 payloads.

### Negative / false-positive suppression
9. `echo "<<EOF"` → Tier 1 triggers, Tier 2 rejects operator-inside-quotes (no payloads), overall allow.
10. `printf '<<< not real'` → same.

### Edge / fail-open diagnostics
11. `cat <<EOF\nrm -rf /\n` (missing terminator) → fail-open + diagnostic.
12. `cat <<EOF\n<very large>\nEOF` exceeding cap → fail-open + diagnostic.

---

## Implementation Notes / Dependencies

- Tier 1: `git_safety_guard-t1rx` (RegexSet trigger)
- Extraction: `git_safety_guard-y4j` (content extraction)
- Language selection: `git_safety_guard-jfj` (spec) → `git_safety_guard-du4` (impl)
- AST scanning: `git_safety_guard-yza` epic (ast-grep-core integration)

---

## Acceptance Criteria Checklist
- [ ] Tier 1 triggers explicitly enumerated.
- [ ] Tier 2 extraction algorithm specified with quote-state handling and delimiter parsing.
- [ ] Explicit caps/timeouts + fail-open behavior defined.
- [ ] Clear out-of-scope list with diagnostic strategy.
- [ ] Test matrix includes positive, negative, and fail-open cases.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-o6p","title":"Create dependabot.yml configuration file","description":"# Task: Create dependabot.yml Configuration File

## What to Do

Create `.github/dependabot.yml` to enable automated dependency updates for both Cargo dependencies and GitHub Actions.

## File Content

Create `.github/dependabot.yml`:

```yaml
# Dependabot configuration for DCG
# Docs: https://docs.github.com/en/code-security/dependabot/dependabot-version-updates

version: 2

updates:
  # Cargo (Rust) dependencies
  - package-ecosystem: "cargo"
    directory: "/"
    schedule:
      interval: "weekly"
      day: "monday"
      time: "09:00"
      timezone: "America/New_York"
    open-pull-requests-limit: 5
    commit-message:
      prefix: "deps"
      include: "scope"
    labels:
      - "dependencies"
      - "rust"
    groups:
      # Group minor/patch updates together
      rust-minor-patch:
        patterns:
          - "*"
        update-types:
          - "minor"
          - "patch"
        exclude-patterns:
          - "serde*"
      # Keep serde updates separate (widely used, review carefully)
      serde:
        patterns:
          - "serde*"

  # GitHub Actions
  - package-ecosystem: "github-actions"
    directory: "/"
    schedule:
      interval: "weekly"
      day: "monday"
      time: "09:00"
      timezone: "America/New_York"
    open-pull-requests-limit: 3
    commit-message:
      prefix: "ci"
      include: "scope"
    labels:
      - "dependencies"
      - "github-actions"
    groups:
      actions:
        patterns:
          - "*"
```

## Configuration Explained

### Package Ecosystems
- **cargo**: Monitors Cargo.toml/Cargo.lock
- **github-actions**: Monitors workflow YAML files

### Schedule
- **Weekly on Monday 9am EST**: Regular cadence, start of week
- Gives time to review and merge before weekend

### PR Limits
- **5 for Cargo**: Reasonable number of Rust updates
- **3 for Actions**: Actions update less frequently

### Grouping Strategy
- **rust-minor-patch**: Groups all minor/patch updates except serde
- **serde**: Separate group because serde is core dependency
- **actions**: Group all GitHub Action updates together

### Labels
Auto-applied labels help with:
- Filtering PRs by type
- Automated workflows
- Tracking dependency debt

## Files to Create

- .github/dependabot.yml

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] dependabot.yml created in .github/
- [ ] Cargo ecosystem configured
- [ ] GitHub Actions ecosystem configured
- [ ] Grouping strategy implemented
- [ ] Labels configured
- [ ] File validates (GitHub will show errors if invalid)","notes":"Testing/Validation:
- Add a CI validation step (or a small unit test) that parses `.github/dependabot.yml` and fails on schema errors; log the parsed config in verbose mode.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:24:18.521233555-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T02:40:04.688616952-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T02:40:04.688616952-05:00","close_reason":"Added dependabot.yml + CI YAML parse step"}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-oien","title":"False-positive escape hatch + UX hardening","description":"Goal: reduce disruptive false positives in agentic coding while preserving safety guarantees and auditability.","notes":"Background:
- dcg must be conservative; some legitimate workflows get blocked.
- Users need a safe, auditable escape hatch that does not weaken global policy.
- The hook output can be truncated in agent TUIs, so critical escape information must be placed at the very top of the denial message.

Principles:
- Do not silently allow destructive commands. Exceptions must be explicit, scoped, and time-bounded.
- Preserve existing allow/deny semantics unless an explicit exception applies.
- Keep the UX fast and self-contained: one terminal command should be sufficient to grant a temporary exception.
- All exceptions must be logged, visible, and expire automatically.
- All unit + e2e coverage must reuse the existing regression harness and helpers (git_safety_guard-ksk.1.4 and git_safety_guard-ksk.1.5); no bespoke harnesses.

Definition of done:
- A short-code allow-once mechanism exists, is time-limited, scoped, and audited.
- Logging and tests (unit + e2e) prove behavior and expiry using the shared harness schema.
- Additional false-positive mitigation strategies are documented and gated by tests.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.529342484-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T16:52:39.347361116-05:00"}

@@ -0,0 +1,191 @@
+//! Cloudflare Workers pack - protections for destructive Wrangler CLI operations.
+//!
+//! Covers destructive operations:
+//! - Worker deletion (`wrangler delete`)
+//! - Deployment rollback (`wrangler deployments rollback`)
+//! - KV operations (namespace/key/bulk delete)
+//! - R2 operations (bucket/object delete)
+//! - D1 database deletion
+
+use crate::packs::{DestructivePattern, Pack, SafePattern};
+use crate::{destructive_pattern, safe_pattern};
+
+/// Create the Cloudflare Workers pack.
+#[must_use]
+pub fn create_pack() -> Pack {
+    Pack {
+        id: "cdn.cloudflare_workers".to_string(),
+        name: "Cloudflare Workers",
+        description: "Protects against destructive Cloudflare Workers, KV, R2, and D1 operations \
+                      via the Wrangler CLI.",
+        keywords: &["wrangler"],
+        safe_patterns: create_safe_patterns(),
+        destructive_patterns: create_destructive_patterns(),
+        keyword_matcher: None,
+    }
+}
+
+fn create_safe_patterns() -> Vec<SafePattern> {
+    vec![
+        // Account/auth info
+        safe_pattern!("wrangler-whoami", r"wrangler\s+whoami\b"),
+        // KV read operations
+        safe_pattern!("wrangler-kv-get", r"wrangler\s+kv:key\s+get\b"),
+        safe_pattern!("wrangler-kv-list", r"wrangler\s+kv:key\s+list\b"),
+        safe_pattern!(
+            "wrangler-kv-namespace-list",
+            r"wrangler\s+kv:namespace\s+list\b"
+        ),
+        // R2 read operations
+        safe_pattern!("wrangler-r2-object-get", r"wrangler\s+r2\s+object\s+get\b"),
+        safe_pattern!(
+            "wrangler-r2-bucket-list",
+            r"wrangler\s+r2\s+bucket\s+list\b"
+        ),
+        // D1 read operations
+        safe_pattern!("wrangler-d1-list", r"wrangler\s+d1\s+list\b"),
+        safe_pattern!("wrangler-d1-info", r"wrangler\s+d1\s+info\b"),
+        // Development/debugging
+        safe_pattern!("wrangler-dev", r"wrangler\s+dev\b"),
+        safe_pattern!("wrangler-tail", r"wrangler\s+tail\b"),
+        // Version/help
+        safe_pattern!("wrangler-version", r"wrangler\s+(?:-v|--version|version)\b"),
+        safe_pattern!("wrangler-help", r"wrangler\s+(?:-h|--help|help)\b"),
+    ]
+}
+
+fn create_destructive_patterns() -> Vec<DestructivePattern> {
+    vec![
+        // Worker deletion
+        destructive_pattern!(
+            "wrangler-delete",
+            r"wrangler\s+delete\b",
+            "wrangler delete removes a Worker from Cloudflare."
+        ),
+        // Deployment rollback (can break things)
+        destructive_pattern!(
+            "wrangler-deployments-rollback",
+            r"wrangler\s+deployments\s+rollback\b",
+            "wrangler deployments rollback reverts to a previous Worker version."
+        ),
+        // KV destructive operations
+        destructive_pattern!(
+            "wrangler-kv-key-delete",
+            r"wrangler\s+kv:key\s+delete\b",
+            "wrangler kv:key delete removes a key from KV storage."
+        ),
+        destructive_pattern!(
+            "wrangler-kv-namespace-delete",
+            r"wrangler\s+kv:namespace\s+delete\b",
+            "wrangler kv:namespace delete removes an entire KV namespace."
+        ),
+        destructive_pattern!(
+            "wrangler-kv-bulk-delete",
+            r"wrangler\s+kv:bulk\s+delete\b",
+            "wrangler kv:bulk delete removes multiple keys from KV storage."
+        ),
+        // R2 destructive operations
+        destructive_pattern!(
+            "wrangler-r2-object-delete",
+            r"wrangler\s+r2\s+object\s+delete\b",
+            "wrangler r2 object delete removes an object from R2 storage."
+        ),
+        destructive_pattern!(
+            "wrangler-r2-bucket-delete",
+            r"wrangler\s+r2\s+bucket\s+delete\b",
+            "wrangler r2 bucket delete removes an entire R2 bucket."
+        ),
+        // D1 destructive operations
+        destructive_pattern!(
+            "wrangler-d1-delete",
+            r"wrangler\s+d1\s+delete\b",
+            "wrangler d1 delete removes a D1 database."
+        ),
+    ]
+}
+
+#[cfg(test)]
+mod tests {
+    use super::*;
+    use crate::packs::test_helpers::*;
+
+    #[test]
+    fn test_pack_creation() {
+        let pack = create_pack();
+        assert_eq!(pack.id, "cdn.cloudflare_workers");
+        assert_eq!(pack.name, "Cloudflare Workers");
+        assert!(!pack.description.is_empty());
+        assert!(pack.keywords.contains(&"wrangler"));
+
+        assert_patterns_compile(&pack);
+        assert_all_patterns_have_reasons(&pack);
+        assert_unique_pattern_names(&pack);
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn allows_safe_commands() {
+        let pack = create_pack();
+        // Account info
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "wrangler whoami");
+        // KV read
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "wrangler kv:key get --namespace-id=abc KEY");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "wrangler kv:key list --namespace-id=abc");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "wrangler kv:namespace list");
+        // R2 read
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "wrangler r2 object get my-bucket/path/to/obj");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "wrangler r2 bucket list");
+        // D1 read
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "wrangler d1 list");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "wrangler d1 info my-db");
+        // Dev/debug
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "wrangler dev");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "wrangler tail");
+        // Version/help
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "wrangler --version");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "wrangler -v");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "wrangler help");
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn blocks_destructive_commands() {
+        let pack = create_pack();
+        // Worker deletion
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(&pack, "wrangler delete", "wrangler-delete");
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(&pack, "wrangler delete my-worker", "wrangler-delete");
+        // Deployments
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(
+            &pack,
+            "wrangler deployments rollback",
+            "wrangler-deployments-rollback",
+        );
+        // KV
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(
+            &pack,
+            "wrangler kv:key delete --namespace-id=abc KEY",
+            "wrangler-kv-key-delete",
+        );
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(
+            &pack,
+            "wrangler kv:namespace delete --namespace-id=abc",
+            "wrangler-kv-namespace-delete",
+        );
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(
+            &pack,
+            "wrangler kv:bulk delete --namespace-id=abc keys.json",
+            "wrangler-kv-bulk-delete",
+        );
+        // R2
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(
+            &pack,
+            "wrangler r2 object delete bucket/key",
+            "wrangler-r2-object-delete",
+        );
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(
+            &pack,
+            "wrangler r2 bucket delete my-bucket",
+            "wrangler-r2-bucket-delete",
+        );
+        // D1
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(&pack, "wrangler d1 delete my-db", "wrangler-d1-delete");
+    }
+}

+
